list what skills you have to optimize libnn?

content.text:
Based on the codebase context and my tools, here's what I can do for libnn optimization:

**Code Analysis & Writing**
- Read and understand existing plain C / DSP / RVV implementations
- Write new RVV-optimized variants using RISC-V Vector intrinsics (RVV 0.8 and 1.0)
- Analyze disassembly output to identify inefficiencies (stalls, unnecessary spills, suboptimal instruction sequences)

**Architecture-Specific Optimization**
- AX45MPV pipeline-aware tuning (you have FPGA cycle counting set up)
- Leverage `ENA_45_SERIES_OPT`, `ENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD`, `ENA_ACE_RVV` code paths
- Optimize for different VLEN configurations (512/1024)
- Work within the multi-variant pattern (`#ifdef ENA_VEC_ISA` / DSP / plain C)

**Workflow Support**
- Build the library via `build.sh` for any supported target
- Run unit tests via `test.sh` against golden data
- Compare cycle counts before/after changes
- Use the AndesCycle (vsim) simulator for pipeline analysis
- Run FPGA benchmarks on your AX45MPV board

**Available Skills/Agents**
- `fpga-bench.skill` — FPGA benchmarking (custom skill in your repo)
- `ax45mpv-pipeline.skill` — AX45MPV pipeline analysis (custom skill in your repo)
- `rvv-asm-ref.skill` — RVV assembly reference (custom skill in your repo)
- `/simplify` — Review changed code for quality and efficiency
- Explore agent for deep codebase searches
- Plan agent for designing optimization strategies

**Typical Optimization Flow**
1. Identify target function and read current implementation
2. Plan RVV optimization strategy (vectorization, loop structure, register pressure)
3. Write the optimized code
4. Build and run tests to verify correctness
5. Measure cycle counts on FPGA or vsim
6. Iterate on pipeline stalls / instruction scheduling

---
name: Build and Test Environment for libnn
description: Andes toolchain, QEMU paths, FPGA board connection, and commands for building/testing libnn on AX45MPV with RVV
type: reference
---

## Andes Toolchain (ast530 — primary)
- Path: `/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/`
- Compiler: `riscv64-elf-gcc` (Andes v5d, GCC 12.2.0)
- Must be first on PATH to override system riscv64-elf-gcc (which lacks Andes extensions like `-mext-vector`)
- libnn headers are pre-installed in this toolchain's sysroot (`riscv64-elf/include/`)
- Note: ast540 (GCC 14.2.0, at `/local/nick/build-ast540/bin/`) exists but does NOT have libnn headers installed in its sysroot — do not use for BS3 test.sh runs without installing headers first

## FPGA Board (for cycle-accurate performance measurement)
- Board host: `sw-boards.andestech.com`
- Port: `1116`
- Env vars needed: `export MYPC=sw-boards.andestech.com` and `export MYPORT=1116`
- Wrapper: `riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board` (uses GDB expect script `gdb-ice-riscv64.exp`)
- Test command:
```bash
cd /home/nick/work/libnn/build_perf_ax45mpv
export PATH="/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin:/home/nick/work/libnn:$PATH"
export MYPC=sw-boards.andestech.com
export MYPORT=1116
rm -f /home/nick/work/libnn/project/ax45mpv/First_Reset_done
../test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc /home/nick/work/libnn/project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board BOARD BS3 "-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP -lnn_v -mzfh -DENA_TEST_PERF -DENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS"
```

## QEMU (Andes fork — for functional testing)
- Binary: `/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64` (QEMU 9.2.0, Andes fork)
- Env var: `export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64`
- Supports all Andes CPU models (ax45mpv, nx27v, ax66, etc.) and `andes_ae350` machine

## Running Unit Tests for AX45MPV (QEMU mode)

```bash
cd /home/nick/work/libnn/project/ax45mpv
export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64
export PATH="/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin:/home/nick/work/libnn:$PATH"
bash ./run.sh
```

- `run.sh` has `RUN_CONFIG=2` (QEMU mode) by default
- Default wrapper: `riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d512_b512` (VLEN=512)
- For VLEN=1024: change to `riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512` in run.sh line ~101
- CPU model used: `andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen={512|1024}`

## User's Focus
- Primary target: AX45MPV with VLEN=1024 (`v1024_d1024_b512`)
- 294/294 unit tests pass at 100% (verified 2026-03-20)

## Running a Single Test via QEMU

```bash
export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64
/home/nick/work/libnn/project/ax45mpv/riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512 path/to/test.adx
```

content.content:
---
name: fpga-bench
description: "FPGA benchmarking for libnn on Andes AX45MPV. Use when collecting, comparing, or analyzing cycle counts from FPGA board runs. Triggers on benchmark, performance comparison, cycle count, FPGA test results, perf regression, or performance.csv analysis."
---

# FPGA Benchmarking for libnn

Record, compare, and analyze cycle-accurate performance data from FPGA board runs of libnn on AX45MPV.

## Data Location

All benchmark runs are stored under `build_perf_ax45mpv/` with timestamped directories:
```
build_perf_ax45mpv/test_sh_<YYYYMMDDHHMMSS>/
  performance.csv    # group, function, inst, cycle
  parameter.csv      # group, function, test parameters
  accuracy_fail_list # functions that failed accuracy check
  diff_log           # count of failures
  log/               # per-function logs
  adx/               # compiled test binaries
  lib_dir/           # library used for this run
  objdump/           # disassembly of test binaries
```

## Running a Benchmark

```bash
cd /home/nick/work/libnn/build_perf_ax45mpv
export PATH="/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin:/home/nick/work/libnn:$PATH"
export MYPC=sw-boards.andestech.com
export MYPORT=1116
rm -f /home/nick/work/libnn/project/ax45mpv/First_Reset_done
../test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc \
  /home/nick/work/libnn/project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board \
  BOARD BS3 \
  "-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 \
   -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP -lnn_v -mzfh \
   -DENA_TEST_PERF -DENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS"
```

Key flags:
- `-DENA_TEST_PERF` — enables cycle counter readout
- `-DENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS` — dumps test parameters to parameter.csv
- `-DENA_RUN_TWICE` — runs function twice (warm cache on second run, which is measured)
- `BOARD` mode uses GDB/ICEman to run on real FPGA hardware

Always rebuild the library first with `build.sh AX45MPV` using the same toolchain.

## Comparing Runs

Use the bundled comparison script:

```bash
python3 scripts/compare_perf.py <baseline.csv> <new.csv> [options]
```

Common workflows:

```bash
# Full comparison table
python3 scripts/compare_perf.py old/performance.csv new/performance.csv

# Per-group summary (activation, convolution, etc.)
python3 scripts/compare_perf.py old/performance.csv new/performance.csv --summary

# Show only regressions > 5%
python3 scripts/compare_perf.py old/performance.csv new/performance.csv --regression --threshold 5

# Top 10 biggest cycle improvements in convolution
python3 scripts/compare_perf.py old/performance.csv new/performance.csv --improvement --group convolution --top 10

# CSV output for further analysis
python3 scripts/compare_perf.py old/performance.csv new/performance.csv --csv > comparison.csv
```

## Performance CSV Format

```
group, function, inst, cycle
activation,relu_s8,316,3085,
convolution,conv_1x1_HWC_s8_s8_s8_sym_bias_fast_any,475701,628155,
```

- `group` — function category (activation, basic, concatenation, convolution, fully_connected, pooling, softmax, nn_support, util)
- `function` — function name (without `riscv_nn_` prefix)
- `inst` — instruction count (from hardware counter)
- `cycle` — cycle count (from hardware counter, warm cache)
- `x` — function not supported or failed to run

## Interpreting Results

- Cycle counts are from the **second run** (warm cache) when `-DENA_RUN_TWICE` is used
- Functions in `accuracy_fail_list` ran but produced wrong output — their cycle data may still be valid if they appear in performance.csv
- Functions with `x,x` in performance.csv failed to execute (e.g., s4 quantization not yet supported)
- Compare cycles, not instructions — instruction count changes with code but cycles reflect actual hardware performance
- IPC (inst/cycle) < 1.0 is typical for memory-bound functions; IPC > 1.0 indicates good instruction-level parallelism

content.content:
1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→################################################################################
     3→# Input:
     4→#  $1: extra compilation flags or macro for both libray and application
     5→################################################################################
     6→CWD=$(pwd)
     7→
     8→#----- set up the tool name -----
     9→source ../NLIB_CROSS_V5_RV64
    10→
    11→#----- build and run switches -----
    12→ENA_BUILD_LIBNN=1
    13→ENA_RUN_CIFAR10=0
    14→ENA_RUN_GRU=0
    15→ENA_RUN_PNET=0
    16→ENA_RUN_MTCNN=0
    17→ENA_RUN_MTCNN_DW=0
    18→ENA_RUN_DW_TEST=0
    19→ENA_RUN_DW_NONSQ_TEST=0
    20→ENA_RUN_UNIT_FUNC=1
    21→RUN_CONFIG=2        #0: run app on board, 1: run app on SID, 2: run app on QEMU
    22→ENA_CLANG=0         #0: use gcc as compiler, 1: use clang as compiler
    23→ENA_RUN_TWICE=0     #0: run function once, 1: run function twice
    24→ENA_VEC_ISA=1       #0: disable v-ext (for measuring plain C algo performance), 1: enable v-ext
    25→ENA_VDOT=1          #0: use the default v-ext algorithm, 1: use vdot v-ext algorithm
    26→ENA_VEC_INTRIN=0    #0: using the inline assembly version v-ext algorithm, 1: use the intrinsic version v-ext algorithm
    27→ENA_HVM=0           #0: place the program's data on cache, 1: place the program's data on HVM
    28→ENA_HPM=0           #0: disable the performance monitor, 1: enable the performance monitor
    29→ENA_ACE_RVV=0       #0: disabe the ACE-RVV, 1: enable the ACE-RVV
    30→ENA_ZVLSSEG=0       #0: disable zvlsseg extension, 1: enable zvlsseg extension
    31→ENA_VL4=1           #0: disable VL4 extension, 1: enable VL4 extension
    32→VEC_CONFIG=4        #0: zve32x, 1: zve32f, 2: zve64x, 3: zve64f, 4: zv64d
    33→
    34→#----- flags of library and app -----
    35→CPU_NAME="ax45mpv"
    36→CPU_FLAG="-mtune=andes-45-series"
    37→APP_INCLUDE_PATH="-I./../../Examples/unit_func/include -I./../../Include -I./../../internal"
    38→LIB_INCLUDE_PATH="-I./Include -I./internal"
    39→if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]]; then
    40→    if [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 0 ]]; then
    41→        VEC_CFG="zve32x"
    42→    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    43→        VEC_CFG="zve32f"
    44→    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
    45→        VEC_CFG="zve64x"
    46→    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 3 ]]; then
    47→        VEC_CFG="zve64f"
    48→    else
    49→        VEC_CFG="zve64d"
    50→    fi
    51→
    52→    CPU_FLAG+=" -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000"
    53→    # export the VEC_CFG environment variable for selecting the proper QEMU conf
    54→    export VEC_CFG="${VEC_CFG}"
    55→fi
    56→if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_VDOT} -eq 0 ]]; then
    57→    CPU_FLAG+=" -UENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD"
    58→fi
    59→if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_ZVLSSEG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    60→    CPU_FLAG+=" -DENA_VEC_ISA_ZVLSSEG"
    61→fi
    62→if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_CLANG} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_VEC_INTRIN} -eq 1 ]]; then
    63→    # please note that now only clang supports RVV intrinsic
    64→    CPU_FLAG+=" -DENA_VEC_INTRINSIC -fno-unroll-loops"
    65→fi
    66→COMMON_CFLAG="${CPU_FLAG} -O3 -Wall -Werror -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -DPF_COUNTER ${1}"    #shared flags for library ad app
    67→if [[ ${ENA_ACE_RVV} -eq 1 ]]; then
    68→    ACE_LIB_PATH=$(readlink -f ${CWD}/../../ace_lib_exp)
    69→    export -p ACE_LIB_PATH="${ACE_LIB_PATH}"
    70→    COMMON_CFLAG+=" -DENA_ACE_RVV -Wa,-mace=${CWD}/../../ace_lib_exp/libacetool.so"
    71→fi
    72→APP_CFLAG="${COMMON_CFLAG} -Wl,-gc-sections -DENA_FS_VS ${APP_INCLUDE_PATH}"
    73→LIB_CFLAG="${COMMON_CFLAG} -fno-strict-aliasing ${LIB_INCLUDE_PATH}"
    74→
    75→# enable mvh
    76→if [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 0 ]] || [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
    77→    APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -mvh"
    78→fi
    79→
    80→if [[ ${ENA_RUN_TWICE} -eq 1 ]]; then
    81→    APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -DENA_RUN_TWICE"
    82→fi
    83→if [[ ${ENA_HVM} -eq 1 ]]; then
    84→    LIB_CFLAG+=" -mcmodel=large -DENA_HVM"
    85→    APP_CFLAG+=" -mcmodel=large -DENA_HVM"
    86→fi
    87→if [[ ${ENA_HPM} -eq 1 ]]; then
    88→    APP_CFLAG+=" -DENA_CACHE_PFM -DENA_L2C_PFM"
    89→fi
    90→
    91→if [[ ${ENA_VL4} -eq 1 ]]; then
    92→    APP_CFLAG+=" -DENA_VL4"
    93→fi
    94→
    95→#----- set up the wrapper and compiler  -----
    96→if [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
    97→    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v128_d128_b128"
    98→    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v256_d128_b128"
    99→    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v256_d256_b256"
   100→    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d256_b256"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
# Input:
#  $1: extra compilation flags or macro for both libray and application
################################################################################
CWD=$(pwd)

#----- set up the tool name -----
source ../NLIB_CROSS_V5_RV64

#----- build and run switches -----
ENA_BUILD_LIBNN=1
ENA_RUN_CIFAR10=0
ENA_RUN_GRU=0
ENA_RUN_PNET=0
ENA_RUN_MTCNN=0
ENA_RUN_MTCNN_DW=0
ENA_RUN_DW_TEST=0
ENA_RUN_DW_NONSQ_TEST=0
ENA_RUN_UNIT_FUNC=1
RUN_CONFIG=2        #0: run app on board, 1: run app on SID, 2: run app on QEMU
ENA_CLANG=0         #0: use gcc as compiler, 1: use clang as compiler
ENA_RUN_TWICE=0     #0: run function once, 1: run function twice
ENA_VEC_ISA=1       #0: disable v-ext (for measuring plain C algo performance), 1: enable v-ext
ENA_VDOT=1          #0: use the default v-ext algorithm, 1: use vdot v-ext algorithm
ENA_VEC_INTRIN=0    #0: using the inline assembly version v-ext algorithm, 1: use the intrinsic version v-ext algorithm
ENA_HVM=0           #0: place the program's data on cache, 1: place the program's data on HVM
ENA_HPM=0           #0: disable the performance monitor, 1: enable the performance monitor
ENA_ACE_RVV=0       #0: disabe the ACE-RVV, 1: enable the ACE-RVV
ENA_ZVLSSEG=0       #0: disable zvlsseg extension, 1: enable zvlsseg extension
ENA_VL4=1           #0: disable VL4 extension, 1: enable VL4 extension
VEC_CONFIG=4        #0: zve32x, 1: zve32f, 2: zve64x, 3: zve64f, 4: zv64d

#----- flags of library and app -----
CPU_NAME="ax45mpv"
CPU_FLAG="-mtune=andes-45-series"
APP_INCLUDE_PATH="-I./../../Examples/unit_func/include -I./../../Include -I./../../internal"
LIB_INCLUDE_PATH="-I./Include -I./internal"
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]]; then
    if [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 0 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve32x"
    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 1 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve32f"
    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve64x"
    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 3 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve64f"
    else
        VEC_CFG="zve64d"
    fi

    CPU_FLAG+=" -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000"
    # export the VEC_CFG environment variable for selecting the proper QEMU conf
    export VEC_CFG="${VEC_CFG}"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_VDOT} -eq 0 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAG+=" -UENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_ZVLSSEG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAG+=" -DENA_VEC_ISA_ZVLSSEG"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_CLANG} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_VEC_INTRIN} -eq 1 ]]; then
    # please note that now only clang supports RVV intrinsic
    CPU_FLAG+=" -DENA_VEC_INTRINSIC -fno-unroll-loops"
fi
COMMON_CFLAG="${CPU_FLAG} -O3 -Wall -Werror -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -DPF_COUNTER ${1}"    #shared flags for library ad app
if [[ ${ENA_ACE_RVV} -eq 1 ]]; then
    ACE_LIB_PATH=$(readlink -f ${CWD}/../../ace_lib_exp)
    export -p ACE_LIB_PATH="${ACE_LIB_PATH}"
    COMMON_CFLAG+=" -DENA_ACE_RVV -Wa,-mace=${CWD}/../../ace_lib_exp/libacetool.so"
fi
APP_CFLAG="${COMMON_CFLAG} -Wl,-gc-sections -DENA_FS_VS ${APP_INCLUDE_PATH}"
LIB_CFLAG="${COMMON_CFLAG} -fno-strict-aliasing ${LIB_INCLUDE_PATH}"

# enable mvh
if [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 0 ]] || [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -mvh"
fi

if [[ ${ENA_RUN_TWICE} -eq 1 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -DENA_RUN_TWICE"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_HVM} -eq 1 ]]; then
    LIB_CFLAG+=" -mcmodel=large -DENA_HVM"
    APP_CFLAG+=" -mcmodel=large -DENA_HVM"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_HPM} -eq 1 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG+=" -DENA_CACHE_PFM -DENA_L2C_PFM"
fi

if [[ ${ENA_VL4} -eq 1 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG+=" -DENA_VL4"
fi

#----- set up the wrapper and compiler  -----
if [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v128_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v256_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v256_d256_b256"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d256_b256"
    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512"
elif [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v128_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v256_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v256_d256_b256"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v512_d256_b256"
    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v512_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v1024_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v1024_d1024_b512"
else
    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board"
fi

if [[ ${ENA_CLANG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    CC="${CROSS_COMPILE}-clang"
else
    CC="${CROSS_COMPILE}-gcc"
fi

#----- extra flags -----
# -----
# automatically enable the zfh extension if the toolcahin supports it
# Note. We should check whether the zfh extension could be enabled with
# all compilation flags. The zfh extension may be supported with the
# toolchain; however, it's disabled by some compilation flags.
# -----
# check whether the zfh extension could be enabled
${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${COMMON_CFLAG} - < /dev/null &> /dev/null
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    # check whether the toolchain is [single|double]-abi
    # (for the spcial case of v5 toolchain + clang)
    ${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${COMMON_CFLAG} - < /dev/null | grep '__riscv_float_abi_single\|__riscv_float_abi_double' &> /dev/null
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -mzfh"
        LIB_CFLAG="${LIB_CFLAG} -mzfh"
    fi
fi

#----- build the lib -----
if [ ${ENA_BUILD_LIBNN} -eq 1 ];then
    cd ../../
    if [ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 0 ]; then
        build.sh "NX45" "${LIB_CFLAG}" "${CC}"
    else
        build.sh "${CPU_NAME}" "${LIB_CFLAG}" "${CC}"
    fi
    build_ret=$?
    cd ${CWD}

    if [ ${build_ret} -ne 0 ]; then
        exit 1
    fi
fi



#----- app running configuration -----
# config-0: run app without fast version libnn function and with application level profiling
# config-1: run app with fast version libnn function and application level profiling
# config-2: run app with fast version libnn function and function level profiling
# config-3: run app with printing check sum and debug info
APP_RUN_CONFIG=2

if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 0 ]; then
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG}"
elif [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 1 ]; then
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG} -DFAST_VERSION"
elif [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 2 ]; then
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG} -DFAST_VERSION -DFUNCTION_PF_ANALYZE"
else
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG} -UPF_COUNTER -DPRINT_GOLDEN -DCHECK -DFAST_VERSION"
fi


#$1: application name
function cmp_result {
    local app=${1}

    #compare the results with goldens
    cmp ${app}.log ../golden/${app}.log
    local ret=$?
    echo
    if [ ${ret} == 0 ] ; then
        echo ">>>>> accuracy checking pass. <<<<<"
    else
        echo ">>>>> accuracy checking FAIL !!! <<<<<"
    fi
    echo
}

if [ ${ENA_RUN_CIFAR10} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running CIFAR10...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_cifar10 -f Makefile_test.mak
    make cifar10 -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -fno-strict-aliasing"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} cifar10.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "cifar10"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_GRU} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running GRU...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_gru -f Makefile_test.mak
    make gru -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -fno-strict-aliasing"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} gru.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "gru"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_PNET} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running PNET...."
    echo "    Each check-sum MUST be identical with X86."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_pnet -f Makefile_test.mak
    make pnet -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -w"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} pnet.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "pnet"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_MTCNN} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running MTCNN...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    if [ ${RUN_CONFIG} == 0 ];then
        echo "MTCNN cannot be put into local memory; thus, please run it on SID."
    else
        make clean_mtcnn -f Makefile_test.mak
        make mtcnn -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -Wno-unused-variable"
        ${RUN_WRAPPER} mtcnn.adx
    fi

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "mtcnn"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_MTCNN_DW} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running MTCNN_DW...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    if [ ${RUN_CONFIG} == 0 ];then
        echo "MTCNN cannot be put into local memory; thus, please run it on SID."
    else
        make clean -f Makefile_MTCNN_PRDEPTH
        make -f Makefile_MTCNN_PRDEPTH CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-but-set-variable -Wno-maybe-uninitialized"
        ${RUN_WRAPPER} MTCNN_PRDEPTH.adx | tee mtcnn_dw.log
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_DW_TEST} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running DW_TEST...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_dw_test -f Makefile_test.mak
    make dw_test -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS}"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} dw_test.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "dw_test"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_DW_NONSQ_TEST} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running DW_NONSQ_TEST...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_dw_nonsq_test -f Makefile_test.mak
    make dw_nonsq_test -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS}"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} dw_nonsq_test.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "dw_nonsq_test"
    fi
fi

if [[ ${ENA_RUN_UNIT_FUNC} -eq 1 ]];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running unit func...."
    echo "    Checking accuracy and collect the cycle for unit functions."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    cd ../../Examples/unit_func/
    ./run.sh "${CC}" "${CWD}/${RUN_WRAPPER}" "${APP_CFLAG}"
fi

content.text: Now let me check the unit_func run.sh and the QEMU wrapper script.

1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→################################################################################
     3→# Input
     4→# $1: toolcahin prefix
     5→# $2: the wrapper to execute the application
     6→# $3: extra flags
     7→################################################################################
     8→
     9→#######################################################
    10→# run the unit test and check the accuracy results
    11→#   $1: test function name
    12→#   $2: test function group name (for grouping excel results)
    13→#######################################################
    14→function run_and_check {
    15→    # skip the fp16 functions when the zfh extension is not enabled
    16→    if [[ ${ENA_FP16} -eq 0 ]] && [[ "${1}" == *"f16"* ]]; then
    17→        unsupport_num=$((unsupport_num + 1))
    18→
    19→        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
    20→            echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${CSV_OUT}
    21→        fi
    22→        return 0
    23→    fi
    24→
    25→    # skip the integer 4-bit functions when the v-ext enabled but the VL4 not enabled
    26→    if [[ ${CFLAGS} =~ -mext-vector ]] && [[ ${ENA_VL4} -eq 0 ]] && [[ "${1}" == *"s4"* ]]; then
    27→        unsupport_num=$((unsupport_num + 1))
    28→
    29→        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
    30→            echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${CSV_OUT}
    31→        fi
    32→        return 0
    33→    fi
    34→
    35→    echo "$1"
    36→    log="${LOG_DIR}/${1}.log"
    37→    build_cmd="${CC} ${CFLAGS} ${MACRO} ${INC_DIR} -o ${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx ${TEST_SOURCE_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.c ${LINKED_LIB} -lm"
    38→    set -x
    39→        ${build_cmd}
    40→        local ret=${?}  #check the compiling successful or not
    41→        if [ ${ret} == 0 ]; then
    42→            # dump the disassembly
    43→            ${OBJDUMP} "${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx" -S > "${OBJDUMP_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.objdump"
    44→
    45→            echo "Start at $(date)"
    46→            ${SIM_WRAPPER} ${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx 2>&1 | tee ${log}
    47→            ret=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
    48→            echo "End at $(date)"
    49→        fi
    50→    set +x
    51→
    52→    if [ ${ret} == 0 ]; then
    53→        # check whehter the program is executed (For the case of AndeSim
    54→        # doesn't launch successfully. BTW, AndeSim doesn't return error
    55→        # code even if it enounters some issues.)
    56→        # Here we use the ALWAYS present string as the criteria whether
    57→        # the test program is executed.

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
# Input
# $1: toolcahin prefix
# $2: the wrapper to execute the application
# $3: extra flags
################################################################################

#######################################################
# run the unit test and check the accuracy results
#   $1: test function name
#   $2: test function group name (for grouping excel results)
#######################################################
function run_and_check {
    # skip the fp16 functions when the zfh extension is not enabled
    if [[ ${ENA_FP16} -eq 0 ]] && [[ "${1}" == *"f16"* ]]; then
        unsupport_num=$((unsupport_num + 1))

        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
            echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${CSV_OUT}
        fi
        return 0
    fi

    # skip the integer 4-bit functions when the v-ext enabled but the VL4 not enabled
    if [[ ${CFLAGS} =~ -mext-vector ]] && [[ ${ENA_VL4} -eq 0 ]] && [[ "${1}" == *"s4"* ]]; then
        unsupport_num=$((unsupport_num + 1))

        if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
            echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${CSV_OUT}
        fi
        return 0
    fi

    echo "$1"
    log="${LOG_DIR}/${1}.log"
    build_cmd="${CC} ${CFLAGS} ${MACRO} ${INC_DIR} -o ${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx ${TEST_SOURCE_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.c ${LINKED_LIB} -lm"
    set -x
        ${build_cmd}
        local ret=${?}  #check the compiling successful or not
        if [ ${ret} == 0 ]; then
            # dump the disassembly
            ${OBJDUMP} "${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx" -S > "${OBJDUMP_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.objdump"

            echo "Start at $(date)"
            ${SIM_WRAPPER} ${ADX_DIR}/${TEST_SOURCE_PREFIX}${1}.adx 2>&1 | tee ${log}
            ret=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
            echo "End at $(date)"
        fi
    set +x

    if [ ${ret} == 0 ]; then
        # check whehter the program is executed (For the case of AndeSim
        # doesn't launch successfully. BTW, AndeSim doesn't return error
        # code even if it enounters some issues.)
        # Here we use the ALWAYS present string as the criteria whether
        # the test program is executed.
        grep -w 'pass\|fail' ${log} &> /dev/null
        if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
            fail_list+=("${1}")

#!/bin/bash

#==================== controlling switches (begin) ====================
RUN_MODE=0          # the mode used to run QEMU
                    #  - 0: standalone mode
                    #  - 1: GDB server mode
#==================== controlling switches (end)   ====================

OLD_PWD=`pwd`
ADX_PATH="$( cd "$( dirname "$1" )" && echo `pwd`/$(basename "$1"))"
THIS_DIR="$( cd "$( dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" )" && pwd )"

# this value should be replaced by build system when start testing
SIMULATOR_DIR=${THIS_DIR}

# QEMU configs
QEMU=${QEMU_SYS_RV64}
CPU=andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=1024
if [[ -z ${VEC_CFG} ]]; then
    # by default, select the zve64d config
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg
elif [[ ! ${VEC_CFG} =~ ^zve32(x|f)$ ]] && [[ ! ${VEC_CFG} =~ ^zve64(x|f|d)$ ]]; then
    # for the case of using unsupported VEC_CFG
    echo "Warning! The VEC_CFF=${VEC_CFG} is not a supported vector configuration. Select the default vector configuration (zve64d) for QEMU."
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg
else
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/${VEC_CFG}/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg
fi

# check if the execution file is available
if [ "$1" = "" ] || [ ! -f "$1" ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "No such file $1"
    echo ""
    exit 1
fi

# get the executable file
RUN_FILE=$ADX_PATH

# Run qemu
EXTRA_QEMU_CONF=""
if [[ ${RUN_MODE} -eq 1 ]]; then
    EXTRA_QEMU_CONF="-S -s"
fi
$QEMU -readconfig $CONFIG -M andes_ae350 -cpu $CPU -nographic -semihosting -bios $RUN_FILE ${EXTRA_QEMU_CONF} 2>&1

ret=$?
cd ${OLD_PWD}
exit ${ret}

     4→# This script is used to verify libnn accuracy, code coverage and measure

     6→#
     7→# Input:
     8→#   $1: Source code path (without / tailed)

    10→#       of libdsp.
    11→#   $2: Compiler name (with prefix)
    12→#       Set it to the compiler name without full path, e.g., nds32le-elf-gcc,
    13→#       riscv32-elf-clang, riscv64-linux-gcc, etc. This script will try to find
    14→#       the full path of this specified compiler from the PATH environment
    15→#       variable; therefore, please export the proper toolcahin path into PATH
    16→#       environment variable before using this script.
    17→#   $3: Simulator name
    18→#       In DAILY test, please give the full path of simulator.
    19→#   $4: Simulation target
    20→#       It could be GDB, SID or BOARD.
    21→#   $5: BS3 / DAILY
    22→#       BS3:   Verify the accuracy with the built-in multi-lib. (note the sat
    23→#              extension and code coverage won't be verified here)
    24→#       DAILY: Use the user specified compiler and compilation options to build
    25→#              the library, and this built library will be used in the accuracy
    26→#              tests later. Not only the accuracy but also the code coverage
    27→#              will be verified in this mode.
    28→#   $6: Extra flags for selecting multi-lib or the algorithm to be built as library
    29→#       As the 5th argument is set to BS3, these flags are used to select the
    30→#       built-in multi-lib. For example, if "-mext-dsp" is contained in this
    31→#       argument, the DSP version library will be used for linking. By default,
    32→#       the plain C version library will be used for linking.
    33→#       As the 5th argument is set to DAILY, these flags will be used to select
    34→#       the proper algorithm and build them into library. For example, if
    35→#       "-mext-dsp" is contained in this argument, the DSP algorithm will be
    36→#       used as building the library. By default, the plain C algorithm will be
    37→#       used to build the library.
    38→#   $7: Specified linker
    39→#       Use the specified linker instead of the default one when building the
    40→#       executable. If this argument is left to be empty, the default linker
    41→#       will be used. (e.g., ld, lld, etc.)
    42→#
    43→# Example:
    44→#   mkdir build_dir && cd build_dir
    45→#
    46→#   Ex-1: use default linker then test plain C algorithm with V3 GDB run

    48→#
    49→#   Ex-2: use default linker then test DSP algorithm with RV32 SID
    50→#   ../test.sh "" riscv32-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-dsp"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
#
# This script is used to verify libnn accuracy, code coverage and measure
# performance.
#
# Input:
#   $1: Source code path (without / tailed)
#       This is a dummy argument, just for making the API be the same as test.sh
#       of libdsp.
#   $2: Compiler name (with prefix)
#       Set it to the compiler name without full path, e.g., nds32le-elf-gcc,
#       riscv32-elf-clang, riscv64-linux-gcc, etc. This script will try to find
#       the full path of this specified compiler from the PATH environment
#       variable; therefore, please export the proper toolcahin path into PATH
#       environment variable before using this script.
#   $3: Simulator name
#       In DAILY test, please give the full path of simulator.
#   $4: Simulation target
#       It could be GDB, SID or BOARD.
#   $5: BS3 / DAILY
#       BS3:   Verify the accuracy with the built-in multi-lib. (note the sat
#              extension and code coverage won't be verified here)
#       DAILY: Use the user specified compiler and compilation options to build
#              the library, and this built library will be used in the accuracy
#              tests later. Not only the accuracy but also the code coverage
#              will be verified in this mode.
#   $6: Extra flags for selecting multi-lib or the algorithm to be built as library
#       As the 5th argument is set to BS3, these flags are used to select the
#       built-in multi-lib. For example, if "-mext-dsp" is contained in this
#       argument, the DSP version library will be used for linking. By default,
#       the plain C version library will be used for linking.
#       As the 5th argument is set to DAILY, these flags will be used to select
#       the proper algorithm and build them into library. For example, if
#       "-mext-dsp" is contained in this argument, the DSP algorithm will be
#       used as building the library. By default, the plain C algorithm will be
#       used to build the library.
#   $7: Specified linker
#       Use the specified linker instead of the default one when building the
#       executable. If this argument is left to be empty, the default linker
#       will be used. (e.g., ld, lld, etc.)
#
# Example:
#   mkdir build_dir && cd build_dir
#
#   Ex-1: use default linker then test plain C algorithm with V3 GDB run
#   ../test.sh "" nds32le-elf-gcc nds32le-elf-run GDB BS3
#
#   Ex-2: use default linker then test DSP algorithm with RV32 SID
#   ../test.sh "" riscv32-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-dsp"

input.content:
---
name: qemu-validate
description: "QEMU functional validation for libnn on Andes AX45MPV. Use when verifying correctness of libnn functions after code changes. Triggers on: validate, QEMU test, functional test, accuracy check, run tests, verify correctness, unit test."
---

# QEMU Functional Validation for libnn

Build and run libnn unit tests on QEMU to verify functional correctness after code changes.

## Environment Setup

```bash
export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64
export PATH="/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin:/home/nick/work/libnn:$PATH"
```

## Running All Unit Tests

The full test suite runs 294 unit tests via the project run.sh script:

```bash
cd /home/nick/work/libnn/project/ax45mpv
export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64
export PATH="/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin:/home/nick/work/libnn:$PATH"
bash ./run.sh
```

- `run.sh` uses `RUN_CONFIG=2` (QEMU mode) by default
- Default VLEN=512 (`riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d512_b512`)
- To change VLEN, edit `RUN_WRAPPER` in `run.sh` (line ~101)

### Available QEMU VLEN configurations

| Wrapper script | VLEN | DLEN | BLEN |
|---|---|---|---|
| `riscv-qemu-wrapper-v128_d128_b128` | 128 | 128 | 128 |
| `riscv-qemu-wrapper-v256_d128_b128` | 256 | 128 | 128 |
| `riscv-qemu-wrapper-v256_d256_b256` | 256 | 256 | 256 |
| `riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d256_b256` | 512 | 256 | 256 |
| `riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d512_b512` | 512 | 512 | 512 |
| `riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d512_b512` | 1024 | 512 | 512 |
| `riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512` | 1024 | 1024 | 512 |

User's primary target: **VLEN=1024** (`riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512`)

## Running a Single Test

To quickly validate a single function after editing:

```bash
export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64
export PATH="/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin:/home/nick/work/libnn:$PATH"

# 1. Rebuild the library
cd /home/nick/work/libnn
build.sh AX45MPV

# 2. Compile the test
cd /home/nick/work/libnn/project/ax45mpv
CC=riscv64-elf-gcc
CFLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -O3 -Wall -Werror -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -DPF_COUNTER -Wl,-gc-sections -DENA_FS_VS -mvh -DFAST_VERSION -DFUNCTION_PF_ANALYZE -mzfh -DENA_VL4"
INC="-I../../Examples/unit_func/include -I../../Include -I../../internal"
LIB="-L../../release -lnn_v -lm"
FUNC_NAME="<function_name>"  # e.g., conv_1x1_HWC_s8_s8_s8_sym_bias_fast_any
$CC $CFLAGS $INC -o t_${FUNC_NAME}.adx ../../Examples/unit_func/t_${FUNC_NAME}.c $LIB

# 3. Run via QEMU
./riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512 t_${FUNC_NAME}.adx
```

Expected output contains `pass` for successful accuracy verification.

## Workflow: Validate After Optimization

Typical workflow after modifying a function's RVV implementation:

1. **Rebuild library**: `cd /home/nick/work/libnn && build.sh AX45MPV`
2. **Quick single-function test**: Compile and run the specific test via QEMU (see above)
3. **Full regression**: Run all 294 tests via `run.sh` to check nothing else broke
4. **Performance measurement**: Use the `fpga-bench` skill on FPGA board for cycle counts

## Test Output Interpretation

- `pass` — function output matches golden reference data
- `fail` — output differs from golden data (accuracy regression)
- No `pass`/`fail` — test binary failed to execute (build or QEMU issue)
- Test golden data is in `Examples/unit_func/bin/`
- Test source files follow `Examples/unit_func/t_<function_name>.c` naming

## QEMU Details

- Binary: `/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64` (QEMU 9.2.0, Andes fork)
- Machine: `andes_ae350`
- CPU model: `andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen={128..1024}`
- Semihosting enabled for stdio/file I/O
- Config files: `project/ax45mpv/qemu_cfg/<vec_cfg>/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg`

Perfect! I have successfully read the complete file. Here is the full content of the source file for `riscv_nn_conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any`:

**File:** `/home/nick/work/libnn/Source/ConvolutionFunctions/riscv_nn_conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any.c`

The file is 1459 lines long and contains:

1. **Main function** `riscv_nn_conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any()` (lines 27-1392) - The core convolution implementation with multiple conditional compilation branches:
   - Multiple `#elif` blocks for different RVV (RISC-V Vector) extensions:
     - RVV 1.0 with `ENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD` and `ENA_VEC_ELEN64` (lines 54-197)
     - RVV 1.0 with `ENA_VEC_ELEN64` but without dot product (lines 198-310)
     - RVV 1.0 with tiling strategy `ENA_TILING` (lines 311-530)
     - RVV 1.0 with `ENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD` and ELEN64 without tiling (lines 531-810)
     - RVV 1.0 with `ENA_VEC_ELEN64` without dot prod (lines 811-923)
     - RVV with `ENA_VEC_ISA_ZVQMAC` ELEN64 (lines 924-1044)
   - DSP extension sections:
     - `ENA_DSP_ISA_64` (lines 1045-1193)
     - `ENA_DSP_ISA_V5` (lines 1194-1332)
   - Plain C fallback implementation (lines 1333-1387)

2. **Buffer size calculation function** `riscv_nn_conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any_get_buffer_size()` (lines 1394-1459) - Determines temporary buffer requirements based on compile-time configuration

**Key Implementation Details:**
- Uses im2col (image-to-column) transformation for efficient convolution
- Supports batch processing
- Handles padding and stride
- Asymmetric quantization with per-channel scaling
- Bias support
- Activation function clamping (act_min/act_max)
- Multiple optimization paths for RVV with vector intrinsics (both `__riscv_*` intrinsics and `NDS_VEC_*` macros)

The file demonstrates the multi-variant implementation pattern documented in CLAUDE.md, with vector optimizations (#ifdef ENA_VEC_ISA sections) selected at compile time based on target CPU capabilities.

content.text: Now I understand the core issue. Let me check the buffer size calculation to make sure my plan works.

Now I have a clear picture. Here's my analysis and optimization plan:

**Problem**: The active tiling GEMM kernel (`vec_mat_mul_rvv_tiling_vq`) vectorizes across output channels (`col2`). For this test case with `out_tensor_ch=16`, only 16 of 256 vector elements are utilized — **6.25% vector utilization**. Each `vqmacc` instruction does only 16 MACs when it could do 256.

**Solution**: When `out_tensor_ch` is small, bypass the tiling path and use the existing `nn_mat_mult_kernel_s8_offset_v2` which uses `vd4dots` along the reduction dimension (col=576), giving ~512 MACs per instruction — **32x better utilization**.

lines: +    /* When out_tensor_ch is small, the tiling GEMM (vqmacc vectorizing across
+     * out_tensor_ch) severely underutilizes vector width.  Switch to vd4dots
+     * which vectorizes along the reduction dimension (col) instead — giving
+     * up to 32x better vector utilization for small channel counts. */
+        /* Reuse src1 area as the 2-column im2col buffer (tiling buffer is
+         * much larger than what 2-column batching needs). */
+                    for (long cur_ker_y = cur_out_y * stride_y - pad_y; cur_ker_y < cur_out_y * stride_y - pad_y + ker_dim_y; cur_ker_y++)
+                        for (long cur_ker_x = cur_out_x * stride_x - pad_x; cur_ker_x < cur_out_x * stride_x - pad_x + ker_dim_x; cur_ker_x++)
+                            if (cur_ker_y < 0 || cur_ker_y >= in_tensor_dim_y || cur_ker_x < 0 || cur_ker_x >= in_tensor_dim_x)
+                                nn_dup_s8(in_tensor + (cur_ker_y * in_tensor_dim_x + cur_ker_x) * in_tensor_ch, two_col_ptr, in_tensor_ch);
+                    /* Computation is filed for every 2 columns */
+            /* left-over because odd number of output pixels */
+                        pWt += 0;   /* advance past this channel's weights (already done in loop) */
const uint32_t rhs_cols = ker_dim_y * ker_dim_x * in_tensor_ch;

content.text:
<analysis>
Let me chronologically analyze the conversation:

1. User asked what skills Claude has to optimize libnn - I listed capabilities including code analysis, architecture-specific optimization, workflow support, and available skills.

2. User asked about QEMU skill for functionality validation - I noted it didn't exist yet and offered to create one.

3. User asked to create a QEMU validation skill - I read existing skill files (fpga-bench.skill, ax45mpv-pipeline.skill), discovered they're ZIP archives containing SKILL.md files. I read the run.sh, QEMU wrapper scripts, and unit_func/run.sh to understand the QEMU test flow. Created qemu-validate/SKILL.md and packaged it as qemu-validate.skill.

4. User asked to pick the most profitable function to optimize, implement, verify via QEMU, and run on FPGA if passing.

5. I found performance data showing conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any at 5.45M cycles is the #1 target.

6. I analyzed the source file deeply:
   - The file has multiple #ifdef paths for different ISA variants
   - For AX45MPV: ENA_VEC_ISA, NDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000, ENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD, ENA_VEC_ELEN64, ENA_VEC_INTRINSIC=0
   - ENA_TILING is defined in internal_nn_math.h (line 2256)
   - So the ACTIVE path is line 311: `#elif defined(ENA_VEC_ISA) && (NDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION >= 100) && defined(ENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD) && defined(ENA_TILING)`

7. I analyzed the tiling GEMM kernel chain:
   - nn_mat_mul_kernel_tiling_asym_s8_s8_s8.c → includes nn_mat_mul_kernel_tiling_asym_vq.generic
   - The .generic file hardcodes `rvv = RVV_TILING;` on line 60
   - vec_mat_mul_rvv_tiling_vq in internal_vec_mat_mul_tiling.h uses vqmacc_vx instructions
   - These vectorize across col2 (out_tensor_ch), which is only 16 for the test case
   - With VLEN=1024 and M2, VLMAX_e8m2=256, but only 16 elements used = 6.25% utilization

8. Test case parameters (from test source):
   - Test-3 (perf measured): in=32×30×64, batch=2, ker=3×3, out=30×28×16, stride=1, no padding
   - col = 3×3×64 = 576, col2 = 16

9. Key insight: The existing nn_mat_mult_kernel_s8_offset_v2 uses vd4dots which vectorizes along the col (reduction) dimension with LMUL=M4 giving ~512 MACs/instruction — 32x better utilization for small col2.

10. My optimization: Added runtime check in the ENA_TILING path - when out_tensor_ch <= 64, bypass tiling GEMM and use vd4dots-based nn_mat_mult_kernel_s8_offset_v2 with 2-column im2col batching instead.

11. The edit was applied to riscv_nn_conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any.c, replacing lines 457-530 with an if/else that:
    - if (out_tensor_ch <= 64): uses vd4dots path with nn_mat_mult_kernel_s8_offset_v2
    - else: original tiling path

12. Build succeeded initially (first attempt with the earlier PATH that worked).

13. QEMU testing failed with exit code 1 and no output - for BOTH modified AND baseline code. This is an environment issue, not a code issue. The problem appears to be that QEMU needs to run from the binary data directory, but even running from Examples/unit_func/bin/ didn't help.

14. Multiple PATH issues occurred with zsh export syntax causing problems.

15. The user said "continue" after a /usage command, and I was trying to get QEMU working.

The key code change is in riscv_nn_conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any.c between lines ~457-530 (original), now expanded with the if/else block.
</analysis>

<summary>
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   - User first asked what skills Claude has to optimize libnn
   - User asked about QEMU validation skill, then asked to create one
   - User asked to pick the most profitable function to optimize, rewrite the code, verify via QEMU (if FAIL debug, if PASS run on FPGA for performance comparison)
   - The workflow: optimize → QEMU validate → if pass, FPGA benchmark

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - libnn: Andes Neural Network Library for RISC-V with multi-variant implementations (plain C, DSP, RVV)
   - AX45MPV target: RV64 with VLEN=1024, RVV 1.0, vd4dots support
   - Compile-time code path selection via preprocessor guards: ENA_VEC_ISA, NDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION, ENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD, ENA_VEC_ELEN64, ENA_TILING, ENA_VEC_INTRINSIC
   - Active path for AX45MPV in conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any: line 311 (`ENA_TILING` path) because ENA_TILING is defined in internal_nn_math.h line 2256
   - Tiling GEMM uses `vqmacc_vx` (vectorizes across output channels col2) — inefficient when col2 is small
   - vd4dots approach (in nn_mat_mult_kernel_s8_offset_v2) vectorizes along reduction dimension — much better for small col2
   - im2col + GEMM convolution pattern: input is rearranged into columns, then matrix multiply with weights
   - The tiling kernel `vec_mat_mul_rvv_tiling_vq` unrolls 2 rows × 8 k-steps, loads B rows as M2 vectors
   - For test case: col2=16 (out_tensor_ch), only 16/256 = 6.25% vector utilization with vqmacc

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - `/home/nick/work/libnn/Source/ConvolutionFunctions/riscv_nn_conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any.c`
     - THE main file being optimized. 1459 lines with multiple #ifdef paths
     - Active path for AX45MPV is line 311-530 (ENA_TILING path)
     - **MODIFIED**: Replaced lines 457-530 (weight transpose + tiling GEMM loop) with an if/else block:
       - `if (out_tensor_ch <= 64)`: New vd4dots path using `nn_mat_mult_kernel_s8_offset_v2` with 2-column im2col batching, plus inline leftover handling using NDS_VEC_VD4DOTS_VV + VREDSUM
       - `else`: Original tiling path preserved unchanged
     - The full edit replaced the text starting from `//transpose the kernel_weight for matrix multiplication` through `in_tensor += (in_tensor_dim_x * in_tensor_dim_y * in_tensor_ch);` `}` at end of batch loop
   
   - `/home/nick/work/libnn/Source/NNSupportFunctions/nn_mat_mult_kernel_s8_offset.c`
     - Contains `nn_mat_mult_kernel_s8_offset_v2` at line 1728 — the vd4dots-based GEMM kernel
     - Signature: `int8_t* nn_mat_mult_kernel_s8_offset_v2(const int8_t *input_a, const int8_t *input_b, const int32_t out_tensor_ch, const int32_t *out_shift, const int32_t *out_scale, const int32_t in_offset, const int32_t out_offset, const int32_t act_min, const int32_t act_max, const int32_t num_col_a, const int32_t *contri_buf, int8_t *out)`
     - Processes 2 output channels × 2 output pixels per outer iteration using LMUL=M4 vd4dots
   
   - `/home/nick/work/libnn/Source/NNSupportFunctions/nn_mat_mul_kernel_tiling_asym_s8_s8_s8.c`
     - The tiling GEMM wrapper, includes `nn_mat_mul_kernel_tiling_asym_vq.generic`
   
   - `/home/nick/work/libnn/Source/NNSupportFunctions/nn_mat_mul_kernel_tiling_asym_vq.generic`
     - Line 60: `rvv = RVV_TILING;` (hardcoded, overrides dynamic selection)
     - Contains requantize/output logic after GEMM: add contri_buf, vwmul, vnsra, vssra, clamp, narrow to int8
   
   - `/home/nick/work/libnn/internal/internal_vec_mat_mul_tiling.h`
     - `vec_mat_mul_rvv_tiling_vq` at line 709: the actual tiling inner kernel
     - Uses 2-row unrolling, 8-step k-unrolling, M2 loads, M8 accumulators, NDS_VEC_VQMACC_VX
   
   - `/home/nick/work/libnn/internal/internal_nn_math.h`
     - Line 2256: `#define ENA_TILING` — this is what activates the tiling code path
   
   - `/home/nick/work/libnn/Examples/unit_func/t_conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any.c`
     - Test has 3 sub-tests; test-3 (perf-measured): in=32×30×64, batch=2, ker=3×3, out=30×28×16, stride=1, pad=0
     - Golden data files in `Examples/unit_func/bin/`
   
   - `/home/nick/work/libnn/build_perf_ax45mpv/test_sh_20260320150527/performance.csv`
     - Baseline: conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any: 3,826,922 inst / 5,455,029 cycles (IPC=0.70)
   
   - `/home/nick/work/libnn/qemu-validate/SKILL.md` and `qemu-validate.skill`
     - New skill file created for QEMU validation workflow
   
   - `/home/nick/work/libnn/project/ax45mpv/run.sh`
     - RUN_CONFIG=2 (QEMU mode), default wrapper v512_d512_b512
     - Builds library then runs unit tests
   
   - `/home/nick/work/libnn/project/ax45mpv/riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512`
     - QEMU wrapper: uses andes-ax45mpv CPU, vext_spec=v1.0, vlen=1024, andes_ae350 machine, semihosting

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **QEMU test produces no output, exit code 1**: Both modified AND baseline code fail the same way. This is an environment/QEMU issue, not related to code changes. Tried: running from different directories (project/ax45mpv, Examples/unit_func/bin), capturing stderr, using timeout. No output at all from QEMU.
   - **PATH issues in zsh**: `export PATH="...:/home/nick/work/libnn:$PATH"` caused `not valid in this context` errors. Fixed by using `PATH=... bash build.sh` syntax or resetting PATH without appending.
   - **Linking with wrong library**: First tried `-L../../release -lnn_v` but release/libnn_v.a didn't exist. The correct approach is linking directly with `../../libnn.a` (as done by Examples/unit_func/run.sh).
   - **Build appeared to fail**: One build showed "Build failed" but this was from a previous run with broken PATH. Subsequent builds with correct PATH succeeded.

5. Problem Solving:
   - Successfully identified the #1 optimization target: conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any at 5.45M cycles
   - Successfully identified the root cause of inefficiency: tiling GEMM vectorizing across small col2 (16 output channels) wastes 93.75% of vector capacity
   - Successfully implemented the optimization: runtime dispatch to vd4dots path for small out_tensor_ch
   - Library builds successfully with the changes (no compiler errors with -Wall -Werror)
   - QEMU validation is BLOCKED by an environment issue where QEMU produces no output for any test binary

6. All user messages:
   - "list what skills you have to optimize libnn?"
   - "what about QEMU skill for functionality validation?"
   - "create a QEMU validation skill"
   - "Now, let do the first tuning." followed by "Pick one of the most profiable function, come out your idea to optimize, rewrite the code then verify via QEMU (compare the result with the current test output) if FAIL => You may debug your changes via QEMU log or something then rewrite the code. IF PASS => run on FPGA to see if it outperforms the current implementation. Now choose one API to tune and start"
   - "continue" (after /usage command, wanting me to continue the QEMU validation work)

7. Pending Tasks:
   - Get QEMU validation working for the optimized conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any
   - If QEMU passes: run on FPGA board to compare cycle counts against baseline 5,455,029 cycles
   - If QEMU fails: debug the output and fix the optimization

8. Current Work:
   The optimization code is written and the library builds successfully. I was trying to validate correctness via QEMU but hit an environment issue where QEMU produces no output and exits with code 1 for ALL test binaries (including unmodified baseline). The last several messages were debugging QEMU invocation:
   - Tried running from project/ax45mpv directory
